mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the effective federal funds rate raise discount rates and tighten financial conditions, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rate increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE above 8.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that reduces household income and consumption expenditure, causing negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based final demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and PAYEMS month-over-month changes shows a t-statistic below 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint signal underperforms a univariate PAYEMS model over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy regime, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL signal reflation that erodes real debt burdens and supports nominal revenue growth, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of months within the zero interest rate policy regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate depressed residential investment and weak downstream demand for construction materials, appliances, and consumer credit, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months when HOUST is below 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth are not significantly greater than those following negative growth according to a Welch t-test with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm that labor market recovery is matched by real output growth rather than cost-push inflation, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS and INDPRO fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and aggregate supply, triggering negative earnings revisions and negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO declines by more than 1.0 percent month-over-month.
